hypotheses.name: Immune gene family clustering AUROC
hypotheses.method: Define 9 immune gene families (AP1, RUNX, KLF, BCL2fam, HLA_I, HLA_II, CCL, TNFSF, IL2_path) from 209-gene vocab. Build within-family vs cross-family pairs (balanced 40 each). Compute AUROC(-d_within, -d_cross) at each of 12 scGPT layers. Per-family breakdown at best layer.
hypotheses.next_action: Test per-family layer curves; check if structural (HLA) vs regulatory (AP1/RUNX) families peak at different layers

hypotheses.name: Dorothea confidence-tier proximity gradient
hypotheses.method: Split 1183 Dorothea pairs in vocab by confidence: high (A/B, n=270) vs low (C/D, n=913). Build null from same gene pool. Compute AUROC(-d_high, -d_null) and AUROC(-d_high, -d_low) at each of 12 layers.
hypotheses.next_action: Fetch Dorothea file with MOR column to test activation vs repression direction polarity; alternatively use TRRUST A/R split

hypotheses.name: Intrinsic dimensionality progression (participation ratio)
hypotheses.method: For 208 named genes at each of 12 scGPT layers: mean-center, compute SVD, derive participation ratio PR=(sum_eig)^2/sum(eig^2) and stable rank. Test Spearman monotonicity with layer index.
hypotheses.result_value: rho=-1.000, p<1e-8; PR layer0=21.07 -> layer11=1.68; StableRank 5.23->1.30
hypotheses.next_action: Bootstrap resample to confirm robustness; compare PR across functional gene subsets; interpret as dimensional collapse / low-rank routing
